name: Pennsylvania Audiologist License
description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Pennsylvania Department of State, Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs

address.description:
Department of State
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Pennsylvania audiologist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for audiologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Pennsylvania?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
